summary: Workspace layout primitive coordinating panel navigation, top navigation, tools, content, shared chrome, and shell shortcuts.
useWhen: Building a dashboard-style workspace with coordinated left navigation, top navigation, tools, and content.
Navigation chrome needs shared styling, gradient alignment, collapse behavior, and keyboard shortcuts.
avoidWhen: The page is a standalone marketing, authentication, or single-purpose layout.
The interface does not use the coordinated CompoMo navigation surfaces.

alternatives.when: Only a top-level section tab row is needed.
alternatives.reason: BarNav can be used without the complete workspace shell.

alternatives.when: Only persistent left-side destination navigation is needed.
alternatives.reason: PanelNav owns left navigation without requiring the full shell composition.

accessibility: Keep destination navigation in PanelNav and section navigation in BarNav.
Do not override browser keyboard chords when enabling shell shortcuts.
Ensure the default slot contains the page main-content landmark.
states: The shell coordinates chrome but the host application owns active destinations, active tools, and route state.
Use one AppShell as the workspace root rather than nesting shells.
responsiveBehavior: The host and shell must fill the viewport for chrome clipping and synchronized gradient sizing.
Panel collapse and tool-drawer behavior should preserve the main content region rather than introducing a second page shell.

customElements: Assign complex navigation item collections as element properties.
react: Use generated wrappers but preserve native slot names on slotted navigation children.
angular: Bind complex inputs after the generated custom elements are registered and preserve slot attributes in templates.
